title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140-190 words.

tasks.title: Email
tasks.prompt:
Your English friend Dana has told you they feel unfit, unmotivated, and find exercise boring. You think joining a local sports team would help. Write an email to Dana encouraging them to give it a try, explaining the benefits and addressing their concerns about not being good enough.

Write your email to Dana.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Hi Dana,

I have been thinking about what you said last week, and I really think joining a sports team could be exactly what you need right now. I know you said you are worried about not being good enough — but honestly, that concern is far more common than you think, and most teams actively welcome complete beginners.

Being part of a team makes exercise so much more enjoyable precisely because you are not doing it alone. You have people relying on you, which provides real motivation, and the social side of it is brilliant — training together and going for food afterwards is genuinely one of the highlights of my week.

Physically, even just two training sessions a week would make a noticeable difference to how you feel. You would sleep better, have more energy, and feel stronger within a matter of weeks.

There is a five-a-side football group that meets on Tuesday evenings — they are completely informal and very welcoming. Why not just come along once and see how you feel? No commitment required.

You genuinely have nothing to lose — give it a chance!

Best,
Finn

tasks.title: Letter
tasks.prompt:
You have read a newspaper article arguing that competitive sport should be removed from school timetables because it causes stress and excludes less talented students. Write a letter to the newspaper editor responding to this argument, explaining whether you agree or disagree and giving reasons and examples to support your views.

Write your letter.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Editor,

I am writing in response to the article suggesting that competitive sport should be eliminated from school timetables. While I appreciate the concerns raised, I believe removing competition altogether would be a serious mistake.

Competitive sport teaches young people skills that are difficult to develop elsewhere: resilience in the face of defeat, how to perform under pressure, and the importance of teamwork. These are qualities that employers consistently identify as highly desirable — and they are developed precisely through the experience of competing, not merely participating.

Of course, poorly managed competition can be damaging. If schools focus excessively on winning at the expense of enjoyment and inclusion, this is genuinely harmful. However, the solution is better coaching and more thoughtful organisation, not the wholesale removal of competition.

Many of the most memorable and formative moments in a young person's school life occur on the sports field. A well-run competitive environment can build confidence and a lifelong love of sport, even among those who do not excel.

I would urge schools to reform their approach to competitive sport, not abandon it.

Yours faithfully,
A Sports Coach

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice on a sports website:

'Share your sporting experience! Write a review of a live sports event or match you have attended, or a sports facility you have used. Tell us about the atmosphere, the quality of the experience, and whether you would recommend it to others.'

Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Wembley Stadium — An Unforgettable Experience for Any Football Fan

Attending a major international match at Wembley Stadium was an experience I had always dreamed of, and I am delighted to report that reality more than matched expectation.

The stadium itself is awe-inspiring. The sheer scale of it — 90,000 seats rising steeply around the pitch — creates an atmosphere unlike anything I have encountered at a smaller ground. When all those supporters sing together, the sound is quite literally physical, and the sense of shared excitement is electric.

Arrangements on the day were impressively well-organised. Stewards were friendly and efficient, queues for food and drink moved quickly, and the seating allocation was logical and clearly signed. Our seats offered an excellent view of the entire pitch.

If there is a criticism, it is that the food and drink prices inside the stadium are eye-wateringly expensive, and transport links on match days can be very congested.

Nevertheless, for any football enthusiast, a visit to Wembley is an absolute must. The combination of sporting spectacle, historic surroundings, and extraordinary atmosphere makes it a truly special occasion.
